citations.desc: Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care (dealers and exhibitors).
citations.narrative:
A female yorkie (#082; DOB 10/20/21), randomly pulled for examination, had two loose upper incisors on the right side of
its mouth. The dog was moving its own incisors with the movement of its own tongue. There was a significant amount of
tartar on both sides of the mouth. Dental issues can be painful and can lead to infection which can become systemic.
Daily observation must be done for all animals to assess their health and well-being and a mechanism of direct and
frequent communication is required so that timely and accurate information on problems of animal health, behavior, and
well-being is conveyed to the attending veterinarian. The attending veterinarian must be contacted and the dog must be
treated as per his/her instructions.

citations.desc: Cleaning, sanitization, housekeeping, and pest control.
citations.narrative:
The feeders had grime built up on the inside of them. The licensee stated they had not been sanitized within the last two
weeks. Not sanitizing the feeders on a biweekly basis allows for grime to build up which can contribute to the spread of
disease. The licensee must sanitize the feeders every two weeks or more often if necessary to prevent an accumulation
of dirt, debris, food waste, and other disease hazards.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the licensee.
End of report.
Additional Inspectors:
Shoshana Tanenbaum, VETERINARY MEDICAL OFFICER n

narrative:
2.40(b)(3)  Direct Repeat
   
Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care (dealers and exhibitors). 
A female yorkie (#082; DOB 10/20/21), randomly pulled for examination, had two loose upper incisors on the right side of 
its mouth. The dog was moving its own incisors with the movement of its own tongue. There was a significant amount of 
tartar on both sides of the mouth. Dental issues can be painful and can lead to infection which can become systemic. 
Daily observation must be done for all animals to assess their health and well-being and a mechanism of direct and 
frequent communication is required so that timely and accurate information on problems of animal health, behavior, and 
well-being is conveyed to the attending veterinarian. The attending veterinarian must be contacted and the dog must be 
treated as per his/her instructions. 
3.11(b)(2)  Repeat
   
Cleaning, sanitization, housekeeping, and pest control. 
The feeders had grime built up on the inside of them. The licensee stated they had not been sanitized within the last two 
weeks. Not sanitizing the feeders on a biweekly basis allows for grime to build up which can contribute to the spread of 
disease. The licensee must sanitize the feeders every two weeks or more often if necessary to prevent an accumulation 
of dirt, debris, food waste, and other disease hazards. 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the licensee. 
 
End of report. 
 
Additional Inspectors: 
Shoshana Tanenbaum, VETERINARY MEDICAL OFFICER n
